findings.title: Potential compute exhaustion from exhaustive fixed-point analysis
findings.description: The SKILL.md instructions recommend an 'Exhaustive' validation mode that rescans the entire codebase multiple times, performs full AST parsing, follows import chains, traces middleware layers, and may run up to 20 passes before convergence. In large monorepos, this can cause substantial CPU and runtime consumption and could degrade availability if invoked without scope limits or user confirmation.
findings.snippet: "Exhaustive level is recommended"; "Re-scan the codebase multiple times"; "The default configuration allows up to 20 passes"; pseudocode shows repeated full discovery in a loop until convergence.
findings.remediation: Set safer defaults (e.g., Standard instead of Exhaustive), require explicit user confirmation for exhaustive scans, cap pass counts conservatively, add file-count/time-budget limits, and support targeted path scoping to reduce resource consumption.

metadata.meta_confidence_reason: The SKILL.md explicitly recommends exhaustive multi-pass rescanning with full AST parsing and up to 20 passes, which can plausibly consume significant compute on large monorepos. The issue is operationally real, though impact depends on repository size and runtime controls outside the skill.
metadata.meta_exploitability: Easy to trigger by running the documented exhaustive mode on a large codebase without limits.
metadata.meta_impact: Can cause elevated CPU usage, long runtimes, and degraded availability for the agent session or host environment.

scan_metadata.llm_overall_assessment: No clear prompt injection, data exfiltration, credential theft, command injection, or external trust abuse was identified in the provided skill content. The primary concerns are operational: the recommended exhaustive analysis mode may be resource-intensive, and the package documentation is incomplete due to missing referenced files and unspecified optional manifest metadata.

meta_correlations.relationship: These findings all concern incomplete packaging/metadata rather than active malicious behavior: missing referenced files and omitted manifest fields reduce auditability but do not create an exploit path by themselves.
meta_correlations.consolidated_remediation: Add a proper YAML manifest with license, compatibility, and allowed-tools fields, and either include the referenced files or remove those references from the package metadata/documentation.

meta_recommendations.title: Bound exhaustive analysis with conservative defaults
meta_recommendations.fix: Change the default depth from exhaustive to standard, require an explicit flag for exhaustive mode, and enforce hard caps such as max files scanned, max wall-clock time, and max passes (e.g. stop after 5 passes or 10 minutes unless the user opts in).

meta_recommendations.title: Add complete manifest metadata
meta_recommendations.fix: Add a YAML front matter manifest to SKILL.md including at minimum: `license: MIT` (or the correct license), `compatibility: Claude Code`/relevant targets, and `allowed-tools: [Read]` or the minimal set actually required.

meta_recommendations.title: Resolve missing file references
meta_recommendations.fix: Either add `chains.py` and `both.py` to the package if they are required, or delete the references so the package contents exactly match the documented components.

meta_risk_assessment.summary: The skill content is largely benign documentation for API contract validation; the only substantive security-relevant issue is potential compute overhead from the recommended exhaustive multi-pass analysis.
meta_risk_assessment.top_priority: Add explicit execution limits and safer defaults for exhaustive scans to prevent unnecessary CPU/runtime consumption on large repositories.
meta_risk_assessment.verdict_reasoning: No evidence of data exfiltration, command execution, prompt injection, credential access, or deceptive behavior exists in the provided skill content. Findings about missing metadata and missing referenced files are transparency/quality issues, not security threats.
